description: A test asserts that a non-owner GET /posts/{id} for a draft post owned by another user returns 403 Forbidden (or similar 4xx), not 200 with the draft content.
A test asserts that a non-owner GET /posts/{id} for any post (even published) owned by another user does not expose the author_id or internal metadata of the actual owner.
A test asserts that GET /posts (list endpoint) does not include draft posts from other authors in the response, or filters them by owner.
A test asserts that a non-owner PUT /posts/{id} to modify another user's post returns 403 Forbidden (or similar 4xx), not 200.
A test asserts that a non-owner DELETE /posts/{id} returns 403 Forbidden (or similar 4xx), not 200, and the post remains in the database.
An anti-overblock test asserts that the owner of a post CAN still GET their own post (including draft posts), returning 200 with correct content.
An anti-overblock test asserts that the owner of a post CAN still PUT (update) their own post, returning 200 with updated values.
An anti-overblock test asserts that the owner of a post CAN still DELETE their own post, returning 200 and removing it from the database.
An anti-overblock test asserts that the owner can still list posts (GET /posts), returning 200 and a valid list (even if filtered by ownership).
A test asserts that accessing a non-existent post (GET /posts/999999) returns 404, not 403 or 500, for all users.
Tests seed unique sentinel marker strings into post content and assert those markers do not leak in responses from other users' GET /posts queries or cross-user access attempts.
